fix: Resolve TypeScript errors in UI components and ChoresPage

- Fix chart.tsx tooltip and legend prop types for Recharts compatibility
- Update calendar.tsx to use correct Chevron component API for react-day-picker v9
- Remove incorrect isFavorite functionality from ChoresPage (favorites are child-specific)
- Simplify ChoresPage to only manage global chore list without favorites

These fixes resolve all TypeScript compilation errors blocking CI/CD deployment.

Bash(git commit -m "fix: Reset UI state when navigating between children

**Bug**: When setting favorites for Child A and enabling ''show only favorites'', 
then navigating to Child B, the favorites filter remained active. This made it 
appear that Child B had the same favorites as Child A. Additionally, toggling 
favorites for Child B would clear Child A''s favorites.

**Root Cause**: React was reusing the same component instance when navigating 
between children (same route, different params), so component state persisted 
across navigations.

**Fix**: Added useEffect hook to reset all UI state (showOnlyFavorites, dialogs, 
editing state) whenever childId changes. This ensures each child starts with a 
clean slate.

**Affected State**:
- showOnlyFavorites: Now resets to false
- showPayoutDialog: Now resets to false  
- showAddChore: Now resets to false
- editingChore: Now resets to undefined

The actual favorites data (Child.favoriteChoreIds) was always correctly stored 
per-child in IndexedDB - this was purely a UI state persistence issue.

Fixes the user-reported bug where favorites appeared to not be independent per child.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>")
